description_long: This object is a short sword or large dagger with a straight, double-edged steel blade that tapers to a sharp point. The blade has a slightly weathered, metallic finish, suggesting it is forged from steel or iron. The hilt features a prominent, curved crossguard with two upward-sweeping quillons, made of a bronze or brass-colored metal. The handle is cylindrical, dark in color, and appears to be made of wood or a similarly dense material, capped with a rounded metallic pommel. The overall design is functional with minimal ornamentation, giving it a utilitarian yet slightly historical appearance. The proportions and materials suggest it is intended for close combat or ceremonial use.
description: A straight, double-edged sword with a metallic blade, curved bronze crossguard, and a dark cylindrical handle with a rounded pommel.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight blade, the curved crossguard, and the cylindrical handle with a metallic pommel.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the circular end of the pommel and the symmetrical curve of the crossguard.
description_view_2: Reverse side view displaying the blade's taper and the alignment of the handle and pommel.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the width of the blade and the shape of the crossguard.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, dark wood or composite, capped with a rounded metallic pommel
